serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Vinegar-Glazed Wood Ear Mushroom with Goji Berries is approximately 120 g, served as a cold appetizer or side dish alongside a main meal.

nutrient_source.title: Mushrooms, wood ear, dried — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168436); supplemented with Auricularia auricula-judae nutritional composition data from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for use in the context of a prepared cold dish with vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, and goji berries
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for this prepared dish exists in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy wood ear mushroom (rehydrated basis) combined with estimated contributions from Chinkiang vinegar, light soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and goji berries at typical recipe proportions. Sodium reflects soy sauce contribution. Vitamin A reflects goji berry contribution. Values are consistent with the input enriched_json and are plausible for this dish type. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mushroom-based and high-fibre vegetable dishes — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. GI of 20 is estimated by analogy with high-fibre, low-carbohydrate mushroom and vegetable dishes documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). Wood ear mushroom is extremely high in soluble dietary fibre and very low in available carbohydrates; Chinkiang black vinegar is documented to attenuate postprandial glycaemic response. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (20 × 9.0g) / 100 = 1.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce marinade
hypertension.guidance: Each 120 g serving provides approximately 384 mg sodium, which is a meaningful contribution toward the 2,000 mg daily limit for hypertensive individuals; portion control and use of reduced-sodium soy sauce are recommended to mitigate this risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and high soluble dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 20 and 4.5 g dietary fibre per 100 g, this dish produces a minimal postprandial glucose response and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided the sugar in the glaze is kept to a minimum.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Polysaccharides, low saturated fat, and anticoagulant properties of wood ear mushroom
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Wood ear mushroom contains beta-glucan polysaccharides and natural anticoagulant compounds that may support healthy blood flow and lipid profiles; the dish is low in saturated fat (0.4 g/100 g) and cholesterol-free, making it a heart-friendly choice when sodium is managed.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidant polysaccharides and carotenoids from goji berries
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Wood ear is rich in anti-inflammatory polysaccharides and goji berries provide zeaxanthin and beta-carotene; however, individuals with RA who are sensitive to nightshade-family plants should note that goji berries belong to the Solanaceae family and may warrant caution.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vinegar-Glazed Wood Ear Mushroom with Goji Berries (凉拌木耳配枸杞, Liángbàn mù'ěr pèi gǒuqǐ) is a classic Chinese cold appetizer and side dish featuring rehydrated Auricularia auricula-judae (wood ear or black fungus) mushrooms. The mushrooms are briefly blanched to achieve a tender yet distinctly crunchy and slightly gelatinous texture, then tossed in a glaze of aged Zhejiang Chinkiang black vinegar, light soy sauce, toasted sesame oil, and a small amount of sugar. The dish is finished with rehydrated goji berries, which contribute a mild natural sweetness, a vivid red colour contrast, and additional antioxidant carotenoids including zeaxanthin and beta-carotene. Nutritionally, the dish is low in calories (approximately 55 kcal per 100 g), very low in fat (2.8 g), and notably high in dietary fibre (4.5 g per 100 g), primarily from the beta-glucan polysaccharides of wood ear mushroom. It carries a very low estimated glycemic index of approximately 20, making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content (320 mg per 100 g) is a consideration for hypertensive individuals and can be reduced by using low-sodium soy sauce. Wood ear mushroom has been studied for its natural anticoagulant properties and potential cardiovascular benefits. In Traditional Chinese Medicine, wood ear is associated with supporting blood circulation and cardiovascular health, while goji berries are prized for eye health and immune support. The dish is fully vegan, vegetarian, and dairy-free, and is commonly served as a starter, palate cleanser, or healthy accompaniment to a larger Chinese meal.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this prepared dish exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy wood ear mushroom data combined with estimated contributions from the marinade ingredients at typical recipe proportions. The GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the absence of a direct approved-source match for this specific prepared dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (wood ear mushroom, dried, FDC ID 168436, rehydrated basis) with estimated contributions from Chinkiang vinegar, light soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and goji berries at typical recipe proportions. No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific prepared dish. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero — this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally absent. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: wood ear mushroom contains negligible vitamin D in its rehydrated and blanched form as consumed in this preparation; confirmed near-zero per USDA FoodData Central data for Auricularia species. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — no hydrogenated fats are used in this preparation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — this is a fully plant-based dish. 3. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this specific prepared dish (rehydrated mushrooms with marinade) is not directly reported in USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168436) or FAO/INFOODS SEA for the combined rehydrated-and-marinated preparation; the value is unavailable because USDA reports moisture for the dried form only and no approved source provides moisture data for the assembled dish at typical recipe proportions. This field is set to -1 per the negative sentinel value policy. 4. saturated_fat_g (0.4 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.1 g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.1 g) are estimated from the sesame oil contribution at typical recipe proportions; these are approximate values. 5. GI of 20 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Sodium of 320 mg/100 g reflects soy sauce contribution and is a key consideration for hypertensive individuals. 7. Goji berries are members of the Solanaceae (nightshade) family; individuals with nightshade sensitivity or certain autoimmune conditions should exercise caution. 8. Wood ear mushroom has documented natural anticoagulant properties; individuals on anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications should consult a healthcare provider before regular consumption.
